title: psoralea fruticans
intro: Psoralea fruticans, also known as Indian turnip, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible taproot. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea fruticans include its ability to support bone health, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including tea, powder, and oil, which are often used to support overall health and wellness.
